projectDescription: LLVM is a collection of modular, reusable compiler and toolchain technologies that provides a framework for building compilers, optimizers, and code generation backends.
keyFeatures: LLVM Intermediate Representation (IR) — a portable, SSA-based assembly language
Modular pass framework for analysis and transformation of code
Target-independent code generation backend with support for many architectures (x86, ARM, RISC-V, AMDGPU, etc.)
Clang — a C/C++/Objective-C compiler frontend built on LLVM
LLD — a fast, drop-in replacement linker
LLDB — a modern debugger using LLVM libraries
MLIR — multi-level intermediate representation for reusable compiler infrastructure
Flang — a Fortran frontend and runtime
JIT compilation support via ORC and MCJIT
TableGen — a domain-specific language for describing hardware targets
publicApiSurface: llvm::Module — top-level container for LLVM IR
llvm::Function — represents an LLVM IR function
llvm::IRBuilder<> — helper for emitting LLVM IR
llvm::PassManager — manages and runs passes on IR units
llvm::AnalysisManager — manages analysis results for IR units
llvm::TargetMachine — abstract interface for machine code generation
llvm::LLVMContext — opaque context for LLVM operations
llvm::Value / llvm::Instruction — base classes for IR values and instructions
llvm::Type — represents LLVM types in the IR
opt — command-line tool to run optimization passes
llc — LLVM static compiler (IR to machine code)
lli — LLVM interpreter and JIT runner
llvm-as / llvm-dis — assembler/disassembler for LLVM bitcode
llvm-objdump / llvm-nm / llvm-readobj — binary inspection tools
